message:
⚡ Lazy-load react-markdown in 3 modal/dialog components (#10889)

Create LazyMarkdown wrapper (React.lazy + Suspense) and convert:
- WhatsNewModal.tsx
- SubmitTab.tsx (feedback form)
- FeedbackDialogs.tsx

These only render markdown after user interaction (opening a modal),
so the ~45KB gzipped react-markdown bundle is deferred until needed.

MemoizedMessage.tsx and mission-control panels keep eager imports
since they're in always-visible surfaces where Suspense flash would
be visible.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <clubanderson@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Andy Anderson <clubanderson@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@ +import { lazy, Suspense, type ComponentProps } from 'react' + +const ReactMarkdown = lazy(() => import('react-markdown')) + +type LazyMarkdownProps = ComponentProps + +/** + * Lazy-loaded wrapper around react-markdown. + * Use in modals and dialogs where the initial bundle cost of react-markdown + * (~45 KB gzipped) isn't justified since content renders after user interaction. + * + * For always-visible surfaces (e.g., chat sidebar), import react-markdown + * directly to avoid a Suspense flash. + */ +export function LazyMarkdown(props: LazyMarkdownProps) { + return ( + Loading…}> + + + ) +}
